core_mechanism: diffusion-driven (Turing) instability of a uniform steady state in a two-component activator-inhibitor system whose diffusive coupling is a graph Laplacian, so that the Laplacian's eigenmodes (indexed by node degree rather than a spatial wavenumber) selectively destabilize the homogeneous state and drive spontaneous differentiation into distinct groups with multistability and hysteresis
brief_reason: The abstract centers on Turing instability of activator-inhibitor dynamics transplanted from continuous space onto a network, where the uniform state destabilizes and nodes of a characteristic degree differentiate into activator-rich/activator-low groups, i.e. a differential-diffusion instability with the coupling operator being the network's Laplacian.
